mnemonics: P(A and B) = P(A) * P(B|A) (Chain Rule) - Think 'A AND then B, given A happened'
Discrete vs Continuous: DICE are DISCrete (you can count the sides), a ruler's length is CONTINUOUS (infinite points between marks)
keyPoints: A random variable is a function that assigns a numerical value to each outcome in a sample space.
Discrete random variables take on a countable set of values (e.g., number of heads in coin flips).
Continuous random variables take on an uncountable infinite set of values (e.g., the exact height of a person).
commonMistakes: Confusing independence (P(A|B)=P(A)) with mutually exclusivity (P(A and B)=0).
Using a probability mass function (PMF) for a continuous variable or a probability density function (PDF) for a discrete one.
